title: Is vanilla extract permissible in food despite containing alcohol?
stanceSummary: Vanilla extract is permissible in food products. While raw vanilla extract contains about 35% alcohol, no food product flavored with vanilla (ice cream, cake, etc.) will be intoxicating. The prophetic rule is that whatever intoxicates in large but normally consumable quantities is prohibited in small quantities. Since no reasonable amount of vanilla-flavored food can cause intoxication, it is halal. The same ruling applies to nutmeg and similar food extracts.
transcript:
## Summary of Yasir Qadhi's Position

In a [2025 Q&A session](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=fABBy2fBcIg), Yasir Qadhi addressed a question about vanilla extract and its permissibility given its alcohol content.

- Vanilla extract in food products is **permissible (halal)**
- Raw vanilla extract may contain ~35% alcohol, but **the final food product** is what matters
- No food flavored with vanilla (ice cream, cake, etc.) will be **intoxicating**
- The prophetic rule: what intoxicates in **large but consumable quantities** is haram in small quantities
- "Large" means what a normal person can reasonably consume (e.g., 10 glasses), not an impossible amount (e.g., 1,000 glasses)
- The same ruling applies to **nutmeg** and similar extracts used in food

---

## Full Lecture Transcript (Cleaned)

### The Question

A brother asked about vanilla extract, specifically whether it is permissible given that it contains alcohol.

### Yasir Qadhi's Response

"I love vanilla. I eat vanilla. **Vanilla ice cream is my favorite.** And especially Madagascar vanilla is the best vanilla."

"It has 35% alcohol" -- yes, the raw extract does. But it does **not** have 35% alcohol when you eat ice cream. No food product that is flavored with vanilla is going to be intoxicating due to the vanilla.

You can eat large quantities of vanilla cake, vanilla ice cream, vanilla anything, and it will **not be even close to intoxication.**

### The Prophetic Principle

The Prophet (peace be upon him) gave us the rule: whatever intoxicates in **large quantities** is prohibited in small quantities.

By "large," we mean what a normal person can **reasonably consume** -- so perhaps 10 glasses would be "large." A thousand glasses is not "large" because it is physically impossible to consume. If something does not intoxicate in 10 glasses but would in 1,000 glasses, we don't judge based on that impossibility -- because at 1,000 glasses you would die before becoming intoxicated.

### The Ruling

Vanilla extract when used in **any food item** is not going to intoxicate at all. Therefore, it is **permissible**.

The same ruling applies to **nutmeg** and similar extracts. Yasir Qadhi has a longer, more detailed lecture available online that explains the fiqh of extraction in depth.
